text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver for your names is the CrowdStrike/Palo Alto blowout quarter - AI-driven cyber demand is the headline. Semis are quiet but holding. Yen at a 40-year low adds macro noise; watch for any dollar-strength ripple into tech earnings estimates.

Your holdings: All scanner statuses show HOLD or NEW - nothing screaming sell. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged semi ETF - on margin that's a 3x leveraged position on margin, so a bad tape day can hurt fast; stop at 200 is wide. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, and leveraged - moves fast and decays; tiny size or it can bite. CRWD (+6.07%) is your best single-name performer; the earnings catalyst just happened, so watch for a fade.

Watch: (1) CRWD price action post-earnings - gap-and-crap risk after a big catalyst. (2) SOXL/SMH for any semi sector follow-through or rollover.

Bottom line: Let winners breathe, but keep SOXL and SPAL on a short leash given your margin exposure.
plays: CRWD: Earnings catalyst just cleared - consider locking in partial gains above current levels; stop at 590 is far away on margin, so tightening to ~720 zone makes sense if you want to protect the 6% gain.
SOXL: 3x leveraged on margin is serious amplification - if semis stall here, consider reducing size; the 200 stop is very wide for your risk profile.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but no fresh catalyst aligns cleanly enough to justify adding leverage to an already concentrated, margined tech book.
